title: Reinforcement Learning: Learning by Trial and Reward
concept: Reinforcement learning trains an agent to take actions in an environment, learning from rewards and penalties through trial and error rather than from labeled examples.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Since unsupervised learning has no answer key, what does it look for instead?

options.text: Patterns and groupings in the data

exercises.explain: Right! With no labels available, unsupervised learning searches for patterns and groupings.

cards.title: Teaching a Dog to Sit
cards.body: You don't hand a dog a rulebook explaining "sit" step by step. You say the command, and when the dog happens to sit, you give it a treat. Get it wrong, no treat. After enough tries, the dog figures out which action earns the reward.

cards.title: What is reinforcement learning?
cards.body: Reinforcement learning trains an agent to take actions in an environment. Good actions earn a reward, bad actions earn a penalty, and through trial and error the agent learns which actions pay off.

cards.title: Falling off a bike, again and again
cards.body: Nobody hands you a diagram of the perfect balance angle before you learn to ride a bike. You wobble, you fall, you adjust your weight next time — and slowly the actions that keep you upright start to stick. That trial-and-error loop is reinforcement learning.

cards.title: Agents, actions, and rewards
cards.body: A video-game AI is the agent. Moving left, jumping, or shooting are its actions. Points gained for winning a level and points lost for a lost life are its rewards and penalties — over thousands of attempts, it learns which actions win.

cards.title: It doesn't get the right answer up front
cards.body: Unlike supervised learning, a reinforcement agent is never told the "correct" action in the moment. It only finds out later, through a reward or penalty, whether its choice was good — then it has to work out which action deserved the credit.

cards.title: Answer key vs. reward signal
cards.body: How feedback differs from supervised learning:

left.text:
Told the correct answer
right away, every time

right.text:
Only gets a reward or
penalty after acting

exercises.prompt: In reinforcement learning, what is the thing taking actions called?
exercises.explain: Right! The agent is the one taking actions and collecting rewards or penalties.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A reinforcement learning agent is told the exact correct action before it acts, just like a labeled example.
exercises.explain: False! The agent only finds out how good its action was afterward, through a reward or penalty.

exercises.sentence: An agent learns which actions are good by receiving ___ and penalties over many attempts.
exercises.explain: Rewards and penalties, earned through trial and error, are what teach a reinforcement learning agent.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes reinforcement learning in action?

options.text: A robot arm that gets a small reward every time it successfully stacks a block, and slowly improves its technique over thousands of tries
A program that sorts emails into groups with no labels
A program trained on photos already labeled "stop sign" or "not a stop sign"

exercises.explain: Correct! Earning a reward after each attempt and improving over time is the trial-and-error loop of reinforcement learning.

exercises.prompt: A trash-sorting robot tries different ways of gripping and dropping items into bins. Each time it sorts an item into the correct bin, it earns a point; each time it misses, it loses a point. Over thousands of attempts, it grips and sorts more accurately. Why is this reinforcement learning rather than supervised learning?

options.text: Because the robot learns from rewards and penalties after acting, not from labeled correct answers given in advance
Because the robot was shown thousands of pre-labeled photos of trash before it started
Because there is no agent taking actions in this scenario
Because the robot never makes mistakes

exercises.explain: Exactly! The robot only learns through points earned and lost after each attempt — that trial-and-error reward loop is reinforcement learning.
